data.title: Japan's Finance Minister Highlights Wage Growth as Key to Sustainable Price Targets
data.description: Japan's Finance Minister, Katsuyuki Katayama, emphasized that while wage growth is not a direct objective of the Bank of Japan, it plays a crucial role in achieving sustainable price targets. According to Jin10, Katayama noted that the central bank's focus remains on stabilizing prices, but acknowledged that wage increases are integral to long-term economic stability. The minister's comments come amid ongoing discussions about Japan's economic strategies and the central bank's policies. Wage growth is seen as a vital component in driving consumer spending and achieving inflation targets, which are essential for Japan's economic health.

data.title: Bitcoin's Potential for Greater Appreciation Compared to Gold, Says Strategist
data.description: Macro strategist Lyn Alden has predicted that Bitcoin may experience stronger price appreciation than gold in the coming two to three years. According to NS3.AI, Alden shared her insights on the New Era Finance podcast, highlighting that gold recently reached an all-time high of approximately $5,608 in January. She noted that the sentiment surrounding gold appears somewhat overheated, while Bitcoin faces unfairly negative sentiment. Alden believes this disparity in sentiment offers Bitcoin better upside potential.

data.title: Bank of Japan Deputy Governor Highlights Forex Impact on Prices
data.description: Bank of Japan Deputy Governor Ryozo Himino has emphasized the increasing influence of foreign exchange movements on domestic prices. According to Jin10, Himino noted that fluctuations in the forex market are now more likely to affect price levels than in the past. This observation comes amid ongoing global economic uncertainties and the potential implications for Japan's monetary policy. Himino's comments underscore the need for careful monitoring of currency trends as they could have significant repercussions on the country's economic stability.

data.title: Jupiter: Launches Jupiter Card with full blockchain integration
data.description: Jupiter announced on its X platform that the Jupiter Card is a fully integrated on-chain card embedded in Jupiter Mobile, allowing users to make purchases using their wallet balance. The card supports all Visa-accepting scenarios and is compatible with Visa Infinite and Platinum virtual cards. Foreign exchange fees for card transactions range from 1% to 1.8%. Funded by USDC, the card aims for seamless settlement and is a fully integrated on-chain card for everyday financial transactions, directly compatible with Jupiter cards.

data.title: The number of non-empty Bitcoin wallets hit a record high, while exchange holdings fell to their lowest level since December 2017.
data.description: Santiment data shows that the number of non-empty Bitcoin wallets has reached a record high, while exchange holdings have fallen to their lowest level since December 2017, indicating rising adoption and increased offline storage.

data.title: Japan's Finance Minister Katayama: Deflationary Pressures Persist
data.description: Japan's Finance Minister, Gaku Katayama, has stated that the country has not yet fully emerged from deflation. According to Jin10, Katayama emphasized the ongoing challenges Japan faces in overcoming deflationary pressures, which have been a persistent issue for the nation's economy. The minister's comments highlight the need for continued economic measures to stimulate growth and achieve stable inflation. Japan's struggle with deflation has been a long-standing concern, affecting economic policies and growth strategies.

data.title: Bank of Japan Deputy Governor Comments on Inflation
data.description: Bank of Japan Deputy Governor Ryozo Himino stated that Japan is currently experiencing inflation, with consumer prices on the rise. According to Jin10, Himino noted that the decision on whether Japan has fully emerged from deflation rests with the government. The remarks highlight ongoing economic challenges as Japan navigates its inflationary environment.

data.title: ADA tokens are now available as a payment option at 137 Spar supermarkets in Switzerland.
data.description: Following its integration with fiat currency deposit and withdrawal platform DFX.swiss, Cardano's native token ADA is now available as a payment option at 137 Spar supermarkets in Switzerland. This integration utilizes DFX.swiss's crypto payment standard, Open Crypto Pay, which already supports payments in Bitcoin, Ethereum, and stablecoins such as USDC, USDT, and DAI. Cardano users scan a QR code with their native wallet to pay directly in ADA at checkout. Merchants ultimately receive Swiss Francs, regardless of the cryptocurrency used by the customer. (Decrypt)

data.title: Bank of Japan Maintains Easing Policy with Gradual Adjustments
data.description: The Bank of Japan's Deputy Governor, Ryozo Himino, announced that the central bank will continue its monetary easing policy while gradually adjusting its degree of leniency. According to Jin10, Himino emphasized the importance of maintaining flexibility in monetary policy to support economic stability and growth. The Bank of Japan aims to balance its approach by considering both domestic and international economic conditions. Himino's statement reflects the central bank's commitment to fostering a stable economic environment amid ongoing global uncertainties.

data.title: EU Bond Yields Converge with Germany's, Suggesting Time for Common Euro Debt
data.description: Bond yields across European Union member states are increasingly aligning with those of Germany. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this trend presents an opportune moment for the introduction of common euro debt. The convergence of yields indicates a growing financial cohesion among EU countries, potentially paving the way for more unified fiscal policies. This development could lead to enhanced economic stability within the region, as member states benefit from shared financial strategies and resources.

data.title: Japan Considers Supplementary Budget Amid Iran Conflict Economic Impact
data.description: Japan's Finance Minister, Katsuyuki Katayama, stated that the government is considering a supplementary budget as a potential measure to address the economic impact of the ongoing conflict in Iran. According to Jin10, this aligns with previous remarks made by the Prime Minister, emphasizing the government's readiness to implement timely actions in response to international developments. The situation in Iran has raised concerns about potential disruptions to global economic stability, prompting Japan to evaluate its fiscal strategies to mitigate any adverse effects.

data.title: Japan's 30-Year Government Bond Yield Rises by 1.5 Basis Points
data.description: Japan's 30-year government bond yield has increased by 1.5 basis points, reaching 3.390%. According to Jin10, this rise reflects ongoing adjustments in the bond market amid broader economic conditions. The movement in bond yields is often influenced by factors such as inflation expectations, monetary policy decisions, and investor sentiment. As yields rise, it can signal changes in market dynamics and investor confidence in long-term economic prospects. The Japanese bond market continues to be closely monitored by investors and analysts for further developments.

data.title: Former Pension Fund President Faces Limited Job Prospects
data.description: Masataka Miyazono, who recently resigned as president of one of the largest pension funds globally, is encountering fewer job opportunities than expected. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that Miyazono's situation contrasts with the usual trend where former executives of such stature have numerous options in the financial sector. The shift in the job market for high-profile financial executives is attributed to evolving industry dynamics and regulatory changes. As the financial landscape continues to transform, the traditional career paths for former pension fund leaders are becoming less predictable.

data.title: UBS Report: Bilibili's Q4 Revenue Surpasses Expectations with Strong Ad Growth
data.description:
UBS has released a research report indicating that Bilibili's revenue for the fourth quarter of last year increased by 8% year-on-year, exceeding both the bank's and market expectations by 2%. According to Jin10, the company's advertising business showed remarkable performance, with annual growth accelerating to 27%, surpassing expectations by 3% to 4%. The gross profit margin expanded by 97 basis points to 37.4%, aligning with forecasts.

The report highlights that Bilibili repurchased $14.7 million worth of shares in the fourth quarter, with a remaining quota of $68.8 million. UBS believes the company should complete the share buyback before the November deadline. The bank anticipates a positive initial market reaction to the strong fourth-quarter results, particularly due to the significant outperformance in advertising revenue, describing the results as high-quality.

UBS maintains a "buy" rating for Bilibili's U.S. stock, with a target price of $40.5.

data.title: Commodity Expert Predicts Silver Surge to $500 Amid Comex Trust Issues
data.description: A prominent commodity expert has made a bold prediction that silver prices could reach $500, citing declining trust in Western exchanges. According to Jin10, the expert highlighted that Comex silver inventories have fallen below 90 million ounces, raising concerns about the credibility of these trading platforms. In 2021, the same expert faced criticism for predicting silver would hit $100, but now asserts that the situation has evolved, warranting a more ambitious forecast.

data.title: Jimmy Lai Chooses Not to Appeal 20-Year Sentence
data.description: Former Hong Kong media mogul Jimmy Lai has opted not to challenge his 20-year prison sentence. Bloomberg posted on X, this decision could solidify the pro-democracy activist's imprisonment for potentially the rest of his life. Lai, known for his outspoken support of democratic movements, has been a prominent figure in Hong Kong's media landscape. His choice not to appeal marks a significant moment in the ongoing tensions surrounding pro-democracy efforts in the region. The implications of his decision are likely to resonate throughout Hong Kong and beyond, as it underscores the challenges faced by activists in the current political climate.

data.title: Planet Midday News
data.description: 1. Bitcoin mining companies have sold over 15,000 BTC since last October, indicating a shift in the industry's holding strategy; 2. The Vancouver city government, after legal review, recommended closing its Bitcoin reserve proposal; 3. Worldcoin's affiliated wallet deposited 6.29 million WLD, worth $2.58 million, into Binance; 4. Vitalik Buterin: Next-generation wallets will deeply integrate AI, effectively eliminating a large number of attacks targeting asset theft; 5. SoftBank seeks a $40 billion loan to invest in OpenAI; 6. 2026 Hurun Global Rich List: 23 people in the cryptocurrency sector will be listed, with CZ ranking 12th among Chinese entrepreneurs; 7. The Russian Ministry of Finance plans to introduce a stablecoin bill, stating that it has "huge potential"; 8. Bloomberg analysts: About half of the Solana ETF inflows came from institutional buyers with assets under management exceeding $100 million.

data.title: US judges held a closed-door meeting on Friday to develop a process for refunding Trump's tariffs.
data.description: A U.S. judge will meet behind closed doors with government lawyers on Friday to work out a process to refund up to $175 billion in illegally imposed tariffs, Reuters reports. One judge has called the meeting a “settlement meeting.” Judge Richard Eaton of the U.S. Court of International Trade will meet with lawyers representing the Customs and Border Protection agency, which is responsible for refunding more than 300,000 importers who paid tariffs deemed unconstitutional. Government lawyers have stated that the process for refunding Trump’s tariffs is unprecedented in scale, requiring the manual review of tens of millions of tariff payment records. While U.S. courts are generally open to the public, judges sometimes discuss scheduling or how to handle sensitive information privately with the parties involved. The court’s website schedule indicates that Friday’s meeting will be a “closed-door meeting.” On February 20, the U.S. Supreme Court ruled that Trump exceeded his authority, and that most of the tariffs were unconstitutional, disrupting key aspects of his economic policies.

data.title: Emerging Asian Stocks See Rapid Capital Outflow Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Overseas investors are withdrawing funds from emerging Asian stocks at the quickest rate in almost four years. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the intensifying conflict in Iran is prompting a global market risk reassessment. This trend reflects growing investor caution as geopolitical tensions rise, impacting financial markets worldwide. The situation underscores the interconnectedness of global economies and the sensitivity of emerging markets to international developments.

data.title: Emerging-Market Equities and Currencies Experience Significant Weekly Decline
data.description: Emerging-market equities and currencies are facing their largest weekly drop since the peak of the pandemic in 2020. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the substantial downturn in these markets. The decline is attributed to various economic pressures and uncertainties affecting global financial stability. Analysts are closely monitoring the situation as investors react to the shifting economic landscape. The downturn has raised concerns about the resilience of emerging markets amid ongoing global challenges. Market participants are advised to stay informed and cautious as the situation develops.
